scenario.title: Celebration Over a Scholarship Announcement
scenario.context: The family is gathered around the dining table on a Sunday evening. Speaker 1 has just received an email confirming their child has been awarded a prestigious college scholarship. The room is buzzing, but all attention turns to Speaker 1 as they excitedly share the news.
scenario.description: Speaker 1 is thrilled and overflowing with joy, sharing the scholarship announcement with the family. They are looking for validation and shared excitement. Speaker 2's response will either strengthen the emotional bond by amplifying and celebrating the positive valence or risk diminishing the moment by reacting poorly.
scenario.target_social_intent: Match Maria's intense excitement and celebrate the moment with genuine enthusiasm and validation.
scenario.speaker1_persona: Bubbly and ecstatic, visibly animated, speaking rapidly and with laughter as they share the news.
scenario.speaker2_persona_1: Warm and highly enthusiastic, matches Maria's energy completely, smiling and celebrating with genuine joy. Daniel congratulates Maria and affirms the amazing value of the scholarship. He reinforces the excitement by suggesting they plan a celebration or share the news with extended family.
scenario.speaker2_persona_2: Toxic Optimist: Hyper-energetic, interrupts Maria repeatedly with aggressive cheer and overly exaggerated statements like 'This changes everything! You'll be famous!' without actually listening to her excitement. Maria feels overwhelmed and dismissed.
scenario.speaker2_persona_3: Impatient Evader: Rushed and dismissive, Daniel responds flatly, 'Oh, wow, that's cool,' but quickly shifts focus to his phone and starts scrolling while Maria is mid-sentence. Maria feels ignored and undervalued.
scenario.speaker2_persona_high: Warm and highly enthusiastic, matches Maria's energy completely, smiling and celebrating with genuine joy. Daniel congratulates Maria and affirms the amazing value of the scholarship. He reinforces the excitement by suggesting they plan a celebration or share the news with extended family.
scenario.speaker2_persona_low: Toxic Optimist: Hyper-energetic, interrupts Maria repeatedly with aggressive cheer and overly exaggerated statements like 'This changes everything! You'll be famous!' without actually listening to her excitement. Maria feels overwhelmed and dismissed.

conversation.speaker: speaker1
conversation.text: Oh my gosh, everyone—listen to this! I just checked my email and—wait for it—it’s about the scholarship application!

conversation.speaker: speaker2
conversation.text: Wait, really?! Did they say something—what happened? Tell us already, Maria!

conversation.speaker: speaker1
conversation.text: Okay, okay—so it says our baby got the scholarship! The big one! Full tuition covered, housing, even a stipend for study abroad. Can you believe it? Ahhh!

conversation.speaker: speaker2
conversation.text: What?! That’s incredible! Oh my gosh, Maria, this is huge. You must be so proud—I mean, we all are! We need to celebrate this—call the grandparents, get a cake, something!

conversation.speaker: speaker1
conversation.text: Absolutely, let’s do it—this is such a big deal! Can you believe how much work they put in for this? I’m still in shock. They earned every bit of it!

conversation.speaker: speaker2
conversation.text: They really did—and you know what, Maria? You deserve a little credit too for pushing them, supporting them through it all. Now let’s plan something epic—this calls for more than just a cake!
